title: Psacalium nephrophyllum
taxonomy: Psacalium nephrophyllum is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the larger group of daisies and sunflowers. Further, it is a member of the genus Psacalium, which includes various species of shrubs and subshrubs. Psacalium nephrophyllum is native to the highlands of South America, where it grows in areas with dry to moist conditions. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native environment.

intro: Psacalium nephrophyllum, commonly known as Mexican silver top, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Psacalium nephrophyllum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Psacalium nephrophyllum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support wound healing, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
